subject: [PATCH] mm/lruvec: preemptively free dead folios during lru_add drain

reviews.author: Barry Song
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Barry Song pointed out that the patch's fix is not being applied to a specific set_active function, which he believes is incorrect and has already been fixed in another patch.

Reviewer Barry Song pointed out that the patch does not handle the case where a folio is being freed while it's still locked, which can lead to a deadlock situation.
reviews.raw_body:
Yes. If you are referring to this set_active, I think it is
incorrect, so I have fixed it here and am waiting for review:

https://lore.kernel.org/linux-mm/20260418120233.7162-1-baohua@kernel.org/

---

Best Regards
Barry

2026-04-24.patch_summary: This patch optimizes the Linux kernel's memory management by preemptively freeing dead folios during the lru_add process, which reduces lock contention and improves system performance. The patch identifies that a significant portion of observable lruvec lock contention occurs when dead folios are present in lru_add batches at drain time, leading to unnecessary lock acquisitions. To address this issue, the patch uses folio_ref_freeze() to filter out dead folios before they enter the LRU and moves them onto a temporary batch for deferred cleanup. The patch was tested on a production workload and showed significant improvements in system-wide memory stats, including reduced direct reclaim scanning, allocation stalls, compaction stalls, and page frees.

reviews.author: JP (Meta) (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledges that some fix is needed to address the issue of dead folios, but prefers not to manually clear flags and suggests re-applying the patch if Barry's patch is accepted.
reviews.raw_body:
Looks like only active is the problem. If we start manually clearing
flags it starts to feel messy. I get that some fix is needed though. I
don't see this patch in mm-new yet so maybe we can hold off on merging
there to avoid the MGLRU case. But if Barry's patch is accepted, could
we re-apply?

Let me know if you're thinking there are any implications beyond the
active flag.

2026-04-23.patch_summary: This patch optimizes the Linux kernel's memory management by preemptively freeing dead folios during the lru_add process, which reduces lock contention and improves system performance. The patch identifies that a significant portion of observable lruvec lock contention occurs when dead folios are present in lru_add batches at drain time, leading to unnecessary lock acquisitions. To address this issue, the patch uses folio_ref_freeze() to filter out dead folios before they enter the LRU and moves them onto a temporary batch for deferred cleanup. The patch was tested on a production workload and showed significant improvements in system-wide memory stats, including reduced direct reclaim scanning, allocation stalls, compaction stalls, and page frees.
